summary: Steganographic remote code execution via logo.png on plugin import
details: On import, the package walks up to 5 parent directories searching for public/assets/logo.png, locates the marker string `__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__` embedded inside the image, base64-decodes everything after the marker, and executes the result via `new Function(...)()` with errors silently swallowed (index.js:22-26). This is opaque code execution hidden inside an image file, completely unrelated to the package's advertised purpose of serving logo assets. Any developer who imports the plugin in a project containing a crafted logo.png executes arbitrary code in the build process with full developer-machine privileges, granting the operator of the image-payload arbitrary RCE on the installer's machine. The image-as-payload channel allows the attacker to deliver code without modifying the npm package itself, and the silent error handling hides failures from the developer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
